signals.title: Grid Flexibility Becomes A Local Resilience Product
signals.summary: Renewables variability, electrification, and price volatility make demand flexibility more practical at household and municipal scale.
signals.implication: Utilities, aggregators, and local authorities can turn flexible loads into resilience capacity before large grid upgrades land.
signals.rationale: Generated from 14 registered source adapters with 18 evidence items. Fallback context is included when a source is unavailable, so the nightly run keeps a complete evidence contract without blocking publication. signals.title: Arctic Optionality Grows Before Routine Shipping
signals.summary: Ice variability, rescue capacity, insurance language, and strategic positioning make the Arctic valuable before it is commercially routine.
signals.implication: Mapping, ports, communications, and dual-use infrastructure become options on an uncertain operating future.
signals.rationale: Generated from 14 registered source adapters with 19 evidence items. Fallback context is included when a source is unavailable, so the nightly run keeps a complete evidence contract without blocking publication. Nightly run nightly-2026-05-22T02-03-07-416Z captured 12 evidence items from 9 source families; 5 source adapters returned live data.
signals.why: This candidate is worth monitoring if search-and-rescue and communications budgets rising in polar regions.

evidence.title: Nuclear marine propulsion
evidence.summary: Wikipedia context: Nuclear marine propulsion is propulsion of a ship or submarine with heat provided by a nuclear reactor. The power plant heats water to produce steam for a turbine used to turn the ship's pro.
